section.heading: Purposes
section.chapeau: Activities conducted under this part shall be designed—

paragraph.content: to enhance the antiterrorism skills of friendly countries by providing training and equipment to deter and counter terrorism;
to strengthen the bilateral ties of the United States with friendly governments by offering concrete assistance in this area of great mutual concern; and
to increase respect for human rights by sharing with foreign civil authorities modern, humane, and effective antiterrorism techniques.

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section 572 of Pub. L. 87–195 is based on section 201 of title II of H.R. 2992, Ninety-eighth Congress, as reported May 17, 1983, and enacted into law by Pub. L. 98–151.

note.heading: Delegation of Functions
note.p: For delegation of functions of President under this section, see Ex. Ord. No. 12163, Sept. 29, 1979, 44 F.R. 56673, as amended, set out as a note under section 2381 of this title.
